news.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Formally Signed — Crude Oil Extends Decline to $74.62 (WTI)
news.what_happened: The US and Iran digitally finalized their interim peace agreement, raising expectations of the Strait of Hormuz reopening for commercial shipping. WTI crude fell to $74.62/bbl and Brent to ~$77.80, continuing the rout from $85+ levels before the deal. Cumulative crude drop since June 14 exceeds 9%.
news.why_it_matters: India imports ~85% of its crude needs — every $5/bbl drop in crude narrows the current account deficit by ~$6-7B annually and eases retail inflation. Jet fuel, synthetic rubber, carbon black, and petrochemical feedstocks all get cheaper. Direct P&L tailwind for airlines, paints, tyres, chemicals, and logistics. Rupee also strengthens (94.40 from 95.88 peak on June 11).

news.headline: US Fed Holds Rates Unchanged — S&P 500 +1.08%, Nasdaq +1.91% on June 18
news.what_happened: The Federal Reserve kept the benchmark rate unchanged at its June meeting. Though the Fed signaled growing hawkish tilt (~half of policymakers favor a hike by Oct 2026), markets viewed the hold as a short-term reprieve. S&P 500 closed at 7,500.58 (+1.08%), Nasdaq at 26,517.93 (+1.91%). Chip stocks led. US markets are CLOSED today (Juneteenth holiday).
news.why_it_matters: Global risk-on lifts FII sentiment toward EM markets including India. Indian IT stocks sold off -1 to -2% on June 18 morning on hawkish fears — with Nasdaq now +1.91%, IT recovery is likely. FII flows expected positive. US closed today = no incremental cue, but the June 18 close sets a strong backdrop.

news.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate at 5.25%, Neutral Stance; India Removes Tax on Foreign Bond Investment
news.what_happened: RBI left repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in its latest meeting, maintaining neutral stance amid cautious inflation outlook. Separately, the Centre announced removal of tax on interest income and capital gains from government securities for foreign investors — India's biggest bond market reform in years.
news.why_it_matters: Rate stability anchors NIM expectations for banks. The bond reform could attract $10-15B in fresh FII flows over coming quarters, supporting the rupee and reducing G-Sec yields — bullish for NBFCs and real estate via lower cost of capital. Short-term liquidity impact on bond markets positive.

news.headline: Nikkei +1.8% Crosses 71,000 for First Time; Asia Risk-On (China/HK Closed for Dragon Boat Festival)
news.what_happened: Japan's Nikkei 225 surged ~1.8% to cross 71,000 for the first time — boosted by the US-Iran peace deal and Nasdaq's surge. Korea's KOSPI also hit record 9,000. Hong Kong and mainland China markets are CLOSED today for Dragon Boat Festival holiday.
news.why_it_matters: Regional risk-on supports FII flows into India. Absence of China/HK markets eliminates a potential drag. Nikkei at record high signals strong global sentiment. Positive backdrop for broad Nifty rally, especially large-cap bluechips with high FII ownership.

news.headline: India Merchandise Exports Up 16.09% YoY in Apr-May FY27 to $88.91 Billion
news.what_happened: India's merchandise exports for April-May FY2026-27 rose 16.09% YoY to $88.91 billion, driven by manufacturing, chemicals, and electronics. This comes even as the rupee has remained structurally weak at 94-96 per dollar.
news.why_it_matters: Strong export performance validates Make-in-India manufacturing momentum and signals competitive pricing of Indian goods globally. The weak rupee has actually aided export competitiveness. Positive macro signal — reduces pressure on trade balance alongside the crude fall.

sector_outlook.reason: WTI crude at $74.62 (-2.5%) — jet fuel is 35-40% of OPEX; massive cost relief
Crude derivatives (40-50% of raw material) cheaper; margin expansion expected
Synthetic rubber and carbon black (60-70% input) linked to crude; tailwind

Lower fuel prices lift consumer vehicle demand; cheaper logistics

ONGC/OIL — lower crude realization; HPCL/BPCL/IOC — lower inventory gains

picks.catalyst: WTI crude -2.5% to $74.62; Brent ~$77.80 on US-Iran peace deal progress
picks.logic: Jet fuel constitutes ~38-40% of IndiGo's total OPEX. Every $1/bbl fall in crude saves IndiGo approximately ₹400-450 Cr annually. With crude down >$10 from pre-war levels (was $85+), this is a ₹4,000-4,500 Cr annual earnings tailwind — transformational for airline profitability. IndiGo also benefits from lower lease costs as global aircraft demand stabilizes. Strong domestic passenger traffic growth reinforces the bullish case.

picks.catalyst: Crude and petrochemical feedstocks cheaper; domestic consumption stable
picks.logic: Crude oil derivatives (titanium dioxide, VAM, ethylene oxide) constitute 40-50% of Asian Paints' raw material basket. With WTI at $74.62 (down from $85+ in May 2026), input costs are set for meaningful relief in Q1/Q2 FY27. Asian Paints already trades at a premium to history on volume recovery expectations — margin expansion from lower raws is a strong re-rating catalyst. Management had guided for margin improvement if crude sustains below $80.

picks.catalyst: Crude-linked synthetic rubber and carbon black prices falling sharply
picks.logic: 60-70% of Apollo Tyres' raw material cost is linked to crude: synthetic rubber (SBR, BR), carbon black, nylon fabric, and process oils all derived from petrochemicals. With WTI at $74.62 (down ~$10+ from May highs), Apollo's input cost basket will deflate substantially in Q1/Q2 FY27. Apollo also has European operations where energy cost relief is amplified. Stock trades at reasonable 12-13x FY27E earnings — the crude tailwind can lift EPS estimates meaningfully.

risks: GIFT Nifty at 24,003 vs Nifty spot close of 24,168 (-165 pts) indicates cautious/lower open — watch 24,000 as key support; breach = step aside
US markets CLOSED today (Juneteenth holiday) — no real-time US cues; global liquidity thinner than usual

Fed hawkish signal (rate hike priced for Oct 2026, US 10Y at 4.46%) — any strong US data could trigger FII outflows from EM next week
Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline uncertain — if implementation stalls, crude can rebound sharply, reversing all crude-sensitive picks
INDIGO/ASIANPAINT/APOLLOTYRE may gap-up at open (crude story is well-known) — avoid chasing; only enter in watch zone
Nifty at 5th consecutive gain — increased profit-taking risk intraday
notes: US markets closed (Juneteenth). China/HK closed (Dragon Boat Festival). Not an expiry day (weekly expiry was yesterday, June 18 Thursday). GIFT Nifty -0.68% vs Nifty spot suggests mild caution despite strong US/Asia backdrop. Dominant theme: US-Iran peace deal = crude crash = broad India bullish.
